name: Li Shan Oolong
terroir: Very high elevation (~1800–2500m), cold mountain air, persistent mist, slow leaf growth concentrates aromatics
flavor_profile: Thick, sweet, and complex — orchid florals layered with creamy milkiness and dried-peach fruitiness. Higher body than most gao shan despite the light oxidation. Substantial huigan that builds across steeps, with melon and snap-pea vegetal notes in later infusions.

brewing.tips: Treat like other unroasted gao shan — 90°C, patient first steep for the ball-rolled leaf to open. Quality Li Shan rewards lighter ratios that let the florals speak.
